Lesser Summon Corpse  

Description

1: Summon Corpse (max level 35)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 100 Skill: Conjuration
Casting Time: 5 Recast Time: 12
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 10000
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 20 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Game Description

Summons your target's corpse to you. This spell works on characters up to level 35. Consumes a black ceremonial coffin when cast.

Reagents

  • Black Ceremonial Coffin x 1

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Lesser Summon Corpse

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Lesser Summon Corpse
ClassLevel
NEC12
SHD12
Expansion:

Duration:
Instant
  • Comments (83)
  • Sold By (11)
  • Screenshots

Merchants:


Abysmal Sea
  • Daris Rodril - 3gp 2sp 3cp

Ak'Anon
  • Clockwork Merchant - 2100, -393, -182 - 3gp 2sp 3cp

East Freeport
  • Malalon Morotia - 3gp 2sp 3cp
  • Peku Croxe - 2pp 9sp 9cp

Neriak Third Gate
  • Misal S`Kor - 2pp 9sp 9cp

Paineel
  • Dovan Sires - 2pp 9sp 9cp

The Plane of Knowledge
  • Heretic Drahur - 3gp 2sp 3cp
  • Reaver Nydlil - 3gp 2sp 3cp

The Qeynos Aqueduct System
  • Gyrna Porrin - 2pp 9sp 9cp

The Rathe Mountains
  • Scribe Noglleg - 2pp 9sp 9cp

West Cabilis
  • Keeper Plight - 3gp 2sp 3cp

# Oct 11 2003 at 3:01 PM Rating: Good

227 posts

▲ Page top
RE: Summoning profits
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2003 at 3:09 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592256700437
Scholar
164 posts
The component you refer to is only used to summon corpses of players under level 35. If a necro is summoning a corpse of higher level player, the component costs 70pp or 150pp depending upon the level of the necro and which spell they are able to use.
▲ Page top
Coffins
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2003 at 5:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593374519737
View User Forum
165 posts
Everyone here is saying stuff like, "I found coffins here!" and "I found coffins at the guy..." All merchants that sell bags, not just backpacks like at general merchants, but all merchants that sell bags & boxes have black ceremonial coffins. For some odd reason, there is no such merchant in PoK, so don't spend all your time wandering the streets like a bum looking for one.

Edited, Sun Sep 7 18:32:46 2003

Scholar

Yes...

And the Coffins you see there are...

Jade Inlaid (Non-stackable)
Jaid Inlaid (Stackable)
Sold by the DE across the pool by the fishing supply vendor

NOT Black Ceremonial....Which can be found in the Bazaar, across from the Trader satchel seller..

# Jul 27 2003 at 9:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966185093

You can purchase this spell in West Cabilis at Keeper Plight (necro spells). My cost was 22 pp 7 gp 3 sp 5 cp for the spell.
▲ Page top
Summon Corpse: Clarified
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2003 at 10:39 AM Rating: Excellent
Caldory
View User Forum
Sage
64 posts
I thought I'd add this for clarification.

Lesser Summon Corpse uses one Black Ceremonial Coffin. Standard price is more than 1pp, but less than 2. Coffins are GIANT in size, and will NOT fit in backpacks etc....

If you cast the spell with no target, you will lose your coffin, and will have no corpse. If you or someone else interrupt you while casting, you will lose your coffin and will have no corpse.

In spite of the yellow spell icon, indicating self only cast in-game, the spell will happily summon any corpse in the zone of the individual you have targetted. This action will also cost one coffin.
▲ Page top
RE: Summon Corpse: Clarified
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2004 at 9:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592263767987
Scholar
39 posts
Toolboxes hold GIANT items. They should hold these, do they?
▲ Page top
RE: Summon Corpse: Clarified
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2004 at 11:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591791380685
9 posts
Most of the items, like the spit, hold giant items. I used my spit to carry the mammoth tusks when I was collecting them in the frosts. I dont see why that wouldnt work too.
Good Luck
▲ Page top
RE: Summon Corpse: Clarified
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2004 at 11:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593363685740
Scholar
55 posts
The reason they cannot be put in backpacks is that they themselves are containers, which cannot be placed into other containers

▲ Page top
Components
QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2003 at 9:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767904810
15 posts
Has anyone ever tried using either a savants cap or a bat skull earring? I have always used these for my pets and it should work for the coffins also. Take for instance the batskull earring it has regent conservation I. This allows you to always use one bone chip for your pet up to lev 24 or something. Then savants cap Regent Conservation II. Uses 1 bone chip for all pets up to 49. Then you get a Iksar Skull Cap Regent Conservation III. I believe it will use one bone chip up to level 60. When these items shimmer you dont use any bone chips. I know what you all are thinking if you are still reading WTF does this have to do with coffins. If your item shimmers while casting summon corpse you have a chance of not using a coffin. You reap the rewards. This also works for peridots and rezes also from what I have been told.
▲ Page top
RE: Components
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2003 at 1:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1704672973637
Guru
5,311 posts
The effect Regent conservation does not guarantee you a savings on components used in spells. It only gives you a chance to not use a regent.

That being said, yes, it Regent Conservation focus effect of the appropriate level would give you a chance to not use a coffin to summon a corpse. Magicians summon necklaces with this effect. It's one of our more underutilized summoned items.
▲ Page top
